marketState: neutral, mixed — near-term weather-driven strength vs. medium-term storage overhang
summary: Price ($3.245 front) sits at a slight premium to modeled fair value ($2.929 now, band $2.429-$3.429), consistent with running +2.3pp above the seasonal median. The near-term picture is genuinely bullish: cooling demand is +15.5% vs 5y in both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows, sitting near the top of its seasonal range (rangePos 0.98/0.89), and the curve is in slight backwardation (front > Sep) confirming near-term tightness. But the medium-term backdrop is weaker — 16-year seasonality gives only a 19% chance of being higher in 4 weeks with a typical -1.8% four-week move (though reliability is flagged Low), storage is +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +13 Bcf injection surprise, and daily/weekly technicals show a bearish 50<200 EMA cross. Managed money is net short at just the 18th percentile of the last 5 years, a crowded short that could fuel a short-covering pop on hot weather but also reflects the market's fundamentally bearish supply view. Net read: fade strength into the medium term, but respect the weather-driven premium over the next 1-2 weeks.
